Learn more about ACCELQ
ACCELQ Automate enhances the efficiency of testing efforts by providing AI-driven automation capabilities. It allows for seamless integration with development pipelines and ensures high-quality software delivery by offering a codeless approach to automation. It supports both web and API testing in a cohesive manner, facilitating a unified testing strategy. Its intelligent features are aimed at reducing maintenance overhead, enabling faster releases, and fostering collaboration among teams. Organizations can achieve scalable automation tailored to specific testing scenarios.
What are the key features of ACCELQ Automate?
Codeless Automation: Enables users to create test cases without programming skills.
Continuous Testing: Easily integrates into CI/CD pipelines for consistent test execution.
AI-Powered Testing: Uses AI to optimize test scenarios and reduce maintenance.
Cross-Platform Support: Offers testing capabilities across web and API.
What benefits should users expect when evaluating ACCELQ Automate?
Enhanced Speed: Faster test creation and execution reduce go-to-market time.
Scalability: Flexible test automation that grows with project requirements.
Cost Efficiency: Minimizes resource requirements with codeless testing.
Improved Collaboration: Facilitates team synergy with easy-to-use features.
In industries such as finance, healthcare, and retail, ACCELQ Automate is implemented to handle unique testing requirements. Its flexible automation approach adapts to specific industry needs, enabling efficient testing strategies. By aligning with industry standards, it ensures high-quality deliverables that meet sector-specific compliance and performance demands.
ACCELQ was previously known as ACCELQ Unified.

Rohit Kumar Majji
Quality Assurance Engineer at Amazon
Verified user of ACCELQ
Jun 28, 2026
Automation has transformed regression cycles and brings QA and non-coding testers together
Author profile
Report as inappropriate
Pros
"We used ACCELQ to automate our regression suite for web and API flows, and it actually helped us cut the regression cycle from about five days to around eight hours."
Cons
"ACCELQ can be improved in a few practical areas. It needs stronger reporting and analytics to help teams get clear visibility into execution trends, failure patterns, and coverage gaps."
What is our primary use case?
My main use case for
ACCELQ
is for end-to-end test automation for web and API testing, especially for CI/CD regression suites.
We used
ACCELQ
to automate our regression suite for web and API flows, and it actually helped us cut the regression cycle from about five days to around eight hours.
Our main use case for ACCELQ, as I mentioned, is to automate web and API regression in the CI/CD flow, with a focus on reducing maintenance and giving the team faster feedback. A specific example is that we used to cut the regression cycle from about five days to around eight hours as I mentioned earlier, and it also made it easier for both automation and manual QA team members to work together.
What is most valuable?
The best features of ACCELQ are its codeless automation, self-healing, and the fact that it brings web, API, and mobile testing into one platform with good CI/CD integration.
The biggest impact for our team is usually self-healing because it cuts down flaky test maintenance and keeps regression runs stable when UI elements change. The CI/CD integration helps by letting you trigger automation as part of the build and release flow, so tests run early, failures are visible faster, and the team gets feedback without manual coordination.
To summarize, self-healing has had the biggest impact for us because it reduced maintenance and made our tests more stable, while the CI/CD integration helps a lot in daily work since we can trigger runs from pipeline and get faster feedback to catch issues before release.
ACCELQ has positively impacted our organization by making our automation more stable, faster to maintain, and easier to scale across the QA team. It also helped us reduce the flaky tests, improve regression turnaround, and bring manual and automation testers onto the same workflow more effectively.
One measurable improvement is that our regression cycle dropped from about five days to eight hours as I mentioned earlier. We also saw a noticeable reduction in flaky test maintenance, which helped the team spend more time on actual test coverage instead of fixing broken scripts.
The platform is especially useful for mixed-skill teams because it lets both QA and non-coding users contribute without making the workflow fragmented.
*Disclosure: My company does not have a business relationship with this vendor other than being a customer.

Business Problems Solved
Recommendations
Business Problems Solved
AccelQ has been widely used across organizations for automation testing, reducing the efforts of manual testers and allowing for scheduled runs of scripts. Users have had an outstanding experience with AccelQ, benefiting from its top-notch support that quickly addresses any issues that arise. The software has helped reduce a 1-hour manual process to just 10 minutes, benefiting the entire team. It is perfect for fast-paced projects, offering both automation and manual testing in the same setup. Moreover, AccelQ is highly efficient for API testing and allows for personalization of parameters. Additionally, users have successfully automated Oracle applications using AccelQ's natural language or low code/no code capabilities. The software is commonly used for automation testing, including regression, sanity, and black box testing. It enables easy test data creation, data verification in databases using SQL, and application of proper validations and checkpoints. AccelQ simplifies data variation automation testing with a few clicks and offers advanced features for working with different aspects of BrowserStack and platform-specific commands. It has been effortless to train Quality Engineering teams with no scripting skills in using AccelQ. Furthermore, AccelQ has helped automate legacy desktop applications that were difficult to automate, even without dedicated automation resources. The software also increases test coverage and reduces technical debts in various environments. Users have benefited from cross-browser testing with AccelQ. Automation testing with AccelQ saves a significant amount of time in creating test cases and scenarios. It has been instrumental in automating functional regression suites, providing early and regular testing feedback. The software seamlessly integrates with other tools like ALM, JIRA, and Atlassian products, supporting web applications, mobile applications, API testing, Salesforce applications, and ERP software. AccelQ is user-friendly and capable, making it a great tool for beginners with no or minimal scripting language while accommodating business logic and simplifying reporting. The product has continuous improvements and proactive support, making it a reliable platform for automation testing. AccelQ is a cloud-based platform that has changed the way users work with test automation. The platform has helped users scale up easily and has provided excellent support throughout their automation endeavors. It has proven to be a valuable tool for QA teams without SDET engineers on staff, as it opens up automation capabilities beyond click and record black box automation. Users have experienced difficulties with other automation solutions when working across multiple platforms, but AccelQ has addressed most of their automation needs. The product has been chosen after conducting POCs and migrating existing scripts based on the positive results and user experience. In addition, AccelQ's automation suite helps identify defects early, keeping the test environment stable and bug-free. Users appreciate that AccelQ is a single tool for both manual and automation testing, making tracking and achieving automation objectives easier. The software has also helped automate functional regression suites, saving time and effort. With AccelQ's automation on the cloud, there is no need for a development environment, allowing for easy script management and server runs. The tool facilitates the creation of proper mind maps, which was previously difficult with large projects. Furthermore, AccelQ simplifies web automation without much coding effort, providing flow visualization and ease of use. Users have extensively used AccelQ for web automation, finding it helpful in reducing maintenance time and improving efficiency. The software offers codeless automation and helps with object identification, API and database automation, reporting, data handling, and cross-browser testing. It seamlessly integrates with other tools like ALM, JIRA, and Atlassian products while supporting various applications such as web applications, mobile applications, API testing, Salesforce applications, and ERP software. AccelQ is a robust tool that supports both manual and automation test execution, as well as tracking test case progress and displaying progress in dashboards. It is considered one of the best tools for beginners with minimal scripting language skills as it accommodates business logic and simplifies reporting. With continuous improvements and proactive support, AccelQ has become a reliable platform for automation testing. Overall, users have had positive experiences with AccelQ due to its user-friendliness, flexibility in handling different types of tests across multiple platforms, and its ability to scale easily while providing excellent support throughout their automation journeys.
ACCELQ Reviews
2 Reviews
Newest First
Filter Results
ACCELQ powerfully propels productivity while championing an AI-first mindset across the organization
Rating: 9 out of 10
Incentivized
July 25, 2025
Use Cases and Deployment Scope
I have used ACCELQ for codeless test automation for one of my client's system. Product was deigned for an air sanitization IOT platform. Product was designed to clean pathogens from air where it is installed. IOT product has to be installed into a room or hall anywhere based on user's choice. The product was backed by a webapp and mobile app UI interfaces over the internet. From UI interfaces user can control IOT device air sanitization scores and space cleaning features. There are separate user interfaces designed for installers, admins and end users of the product.
Pros
Codeless test automtion
Desktop local webapp automation of unit testing
Multi channel automation for an IOT platform product
Autonomous healing with auto pilot
Microservices API automation before deployment of services into production
Email automation for alerts and events into aws IOT platform
Cons
Visual regression features
Test generation from UI and UX platforms like Figma
Manual test tools can be more integrated with AutoPilot
Likelihood to Recommend
Low code test automation, Ready to pickup platform without having much prior knowledge on automation, AI agent interactions are nearly close to real life scenarios, best API automation scale it has got, QGPT logic builder has really changed the talk with DBs in AI way, Logic insights feature is really impressive to identify possible risk while just started developing web apps.

ACCELQ test automation tool
Rating: 7 out of 10
Incentivized
December 6, 2022
Use Cases and Deployment Scope
Our organization has been going through a digital transformation, and as part of that, we were looking for an automation tool that can work across multiple platforms such as mainframe, web, API, etc.; our existing solutions, including open-source selenium, were not working in this situation and hence we were looking for one solution that can address most of the automation needs. Piloted four solutions, including ACCELQ, and conducted POCs, including migration of existing scripts. Based on the POC results and user experience, we picked ACCELQ as the solution to move forward with.
Pros
Scriptless and hence coding is easy.
Maintenance of the scripts are easy.
Learning curve is small.
Cons
The tool is not developer friendly and hence adoption across developers is low.
The tool does not have an admin console to manage the users centrally.
Different types of licensing and it’s all user based and hence pricey.
Likelihood to Recommend
ACCELQ can support multiple technologies such as web, mobile, API, and mainframe. It’s also suited for SAAS solutions such as Salesforce and addresses challenges such as dynamic HTML. It’s easy to set up, and onboarding is easy, and overall lead time is comparatively less. The overall execution results are captured with screenshots, and it’s easy to debug errors. It has integrations with leading cloud-based desktop and mobile farm services such as Saucelabs, browser stack, etc.; ACCELQ is not developer friendly, and hence the overall adoption for a continuous integration scenario is very limited. If you are using a different test management solution, the integration between accelQ and that tool needs ti to be built and hence requires additional development effort, and it’s buggy too.
